From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Basketball team in Yaoundé, Cameroon

| FAP | |
| --- | --- |
| [FAP logo](/wiki/File:FAP_Basketball_logo.png "FAP logo") | |

| History | **Forces Armées et Police Basketball** 1972–present |

|  | |
| |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | | Home jersey    Team colours  **[Home](/wiki/Basketball_uniform "Basketball uniform")** | Away jersey    Team colours  **[Away](/wiki/Away_colours "Away colours")** | Third jersey    Team colours  **[Third](/wiki/Third_jersey "Third jersey")** | | | |
|  | |

**Forces Armées et Police Basketball** (transl. Armed Forces and Police), commonly known as simply **FAP** or **FAP Yaoundé**, is a [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") team based in [Yaoundé](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9 "Yaoundé"), Cameroon. It is the basketball team of the Cameroonian [armed forces](/wiki/Cameroon_Armed_Forces "Cameroon Armed Forces") and police. The team previously played in the [Basketball Africa League](/wiki/Basketball_Africa_League "Basketball Africa League") (BAL) and domestically plays in the [Elite Messieurs](/wiki/Elite_Messieurs "Elite Messieurs"). FAP has won the national championship four times, the [Cameroon Cup](/wiki/Cameroonian_Basketball_Cup "Cameroonian Basketball Cup") twice, and the Central Region League Championship five times.

The [Yaoundé Multipurpose Sports Complex](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9_Multipurpose_Sports_Complex "Yaoundé Multipurpose Sports Complex") serves as home arena of the team.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FAP_Basketball&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The club was established in 1972 in [Yaoundé](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9 "Yaoundé").[[2]](#cite_note-bal-2) In November 2019, FAP entered the [qualifying tournaments](/wiki/2020_BAL_Qualifying_Tournaments "2020 BAL Qualifying Tournaments") for the newly established [Basketball Africa League](/wiki/Basketball_Africa_League "Basketball Africa League") (BAL), representing Cameroon as runners-up because champions Condor BC refused its invitation.[[3]](#cite_note-3) After a good first round run, FAP played even better in the second round. After beating [ABC](/wiki/Abidjan_Basket_Club "Abidjan Basket Club") in the semi-finals, FAP qualified for the first edition of the BAL.[[4]](#cite_note-gspfap-4)

In 2020, FAP won its first silverware after winning the [Elite Messieurs](/wiki/Elite_Messieurs "Elite Messieurs") title. It went unbeaten in the Final Four tournament with a 6–0 record.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Ahead of the inaugural BAL season in May 2021, FAP signed [Lazare Adingono](/wiki/Lazare_Adingono "Lazare Adingono") as head coach.[[6]](#cite_note-6) In the following season, FAP repeated as champions.

In the [2022 BAL season](/wiki/2022_BAL_season "2022 BAL season"), FAP finished in fourth place after eliminating the favoured Rwandan hosts [REG](/wiki/REG_BBC "REG BBC") in the quarter-finals. The third place game was lost to [Zamalek](/wiki/Zamalek_SC_Basketball "Zamalek SC Basketball").

## Arena

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FAP_Basketball&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Arena")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/04/Yaound%C3%A9_Sports_Palace_2014_%2812%29.JPG/250px-Yaound%C3%A9_Sports_Palace_2014_%2812%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:Yaound%C3%A9_Sports_Palace_2014_(12).JPG)

Interior view of the [Yaoundé Multipurpose Sports Complex](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9_Multipurpose_Sports_Complex "Yaoundé Multipurpose Sports Complex"), home arena of FAP

The [Yaoundé Multipurpose Sports Complex](/wiki/Yaound%C3%A9_Multipurpose_Sports_Complex "Yaoundé Multipurpose Sports Complex"), built in 2009, is the home arena of FAP Basketball. Most basketball games of the [Elite Messieurs](/wiki/Elite_Messieurs "Elite Messieurs") final stages are played in the complex.

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FAP_Basketball&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Honours")]

### Domestic

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FAP_Basketball&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Domestic")]

**[Elite Messieurs](/wiki/Elite_Messieurs "Elite Messieurs")**

* **Champions (4):** 2019–20, 2020–21, 2021–22, 2022–23
  + Runners-up (1): 2018–19

**[Cameroonian Cup](/wiki/Cameroonian_Basketball_Cup "Cameroonian Basketball Cup")**

* **Winners (2):** 2022, 2024[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### Regional

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FAP_Basketball&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Regional")]

**Central Region League Championship**[[2]](#cite_note-bal-2)

* **Champions (5):** 2011, 2016, 2018, 2021, 2022

### International

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FAP_Basketball&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: International")]

**[Basketball Africa League](/wiki/Basketball_Africa_League "Basketball Africa League")**

* Fourth place (1): [2022](/wiki/2022_BAL_season "2022 BAL season")
* Quarter-finalist (1): [2021](/wiki/2021_BAL_season "2021 BAL season")

**[BAL Qualifying Tournaments](/wiki/BAL_Qualifying_Tournaments "BAL Qualifying Tournaments")**

* **Winners (1):** [2022](/wiki/2022_BAL_qualification "2022 BAL qualification") (West Division)
  + Runners-up (1): [2021](/wiki/2021_BAL_qualification "2021 BAL qualification") (West Division)
